message:
test(capture-record): wire-format conformance harness for Recording decoder

## Why

The capture-record binary wire format that backs .war recordings is
parsed by `replay::parse_frame` / `Recording::from_bytes`. Until now
every test of that decoder has routed through the *encoder* first,
which means any bug that is symmetric between encode and decode
(e.g. a wrong field ordering, an endianness flip, a miscalculated
header length) would pass round-trip tests silently while breaking
third-party readers of the format.

This conformance harness fixes that by hand-building wire bytes and
feeding them straight to the decoder. It treats the wire spec as the
contract:

  timestamp_ms : u64 little-endian
  frame_type   : u8  (1=Output, 2=Resize, 3=Event, 4=Marker, 5=Input)
  flags        : u8
  payload_len  : u32 little-endian
  payload      : [u8; payload_len]

## What the harness covers

Positive shape assertions:
  - Well-formed frames of each FrameType decode with the right
    FrameType, payload bytes, and timestamp.
  - FrameType::Input round-trips its payload unchanged (previously
    only covered transitively through the encoder).

Negative / adversarial shapes (where hand-built bytes are the only
way to exercise the path):
  - Unknown frame_type tag is rejected rather than silently coerced.
  - payload_len that overruns the buffer is rejected instead of
    reading past end.
  - Truncated headers are rejected cleanly.

No production code is touched; this is a decoder-only coverage
expansion that pins the wire format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
